name: Anthropology
description: This is the behavioral study of man and other intelligent life forms and their environments. Studies include societies, customs and beliefs, religions, political structure, as well as rudimentary history and archaeological background.

narrativeAdvantages.summary: More concerned with the study of modern races and societies than ancient ones—especially useful when dealing with other races and cultures in the present time to avoid accidentally breaking taboos or codes of behavior.
narrativeAdvantages.appliesWhen: When assessing living cultures, customs, or present-day encounters.

narrativeAdvantages.summary: Can indicate whether the anthropologist may be dealing with a dangerous people (hate humans, fear technology or magic, are cannibals, worship demons, etc.).
narrativeAdvantages.appliesWhen: When evaluating an unfamiliar people or society.

successOutcomes: When examining items, artifacts, or ruins, a successful roll can identify the probable people/race, purpose, technological level, and period in which the item was used (contemporary or ancient).

interdependentKnowledge.summary: Includes a rudimentary history and archaeological background.

situationalRuleOverrides.summary: The character is not skilled enough to tell whether an item is an authentic artifact or a forgery.

name: Archaeology
description: This is the scientific study of relics of ancient civilizations by excavation and analysis of artifacts.

narrativeAdvantages.summary: Covers proper excavation (digs) techniques, preservation, restoration, and dating methods (including carbon dating).

interdependentKnowledge.summary: Includes a rudimentary history and anthropology background.

name: Astronomy

synergies.description: Complements pilot and instrument navigation when celestial references are available.
Complements land navigation when celestial references are available.

description: The study of the stars and other celestial bodies, their movements, positions, cycles, alignments, and interrelations with the earth and each other.

narrativeAdvantages.summary: Can be used to determine direction, approximate distances, identify seasons, and tell the time of day.
narrativeAdvantages.appliesWhen: When applying astronomical knowledge in the field.

name: Biology
description: Basic understanding of cells, anatomy, physiology, evolution, and genetics.

narrativeAdvantages.summary: Knows how to use a microscope, cultivate bacteria, and classify new organisms.

name: Botany
description: Plants, their categories and functions, are studied extensively.

narrativeAdvantages.summary: Knows the basics of cross-fertilization and germinating, as well as growing experimental plants.

name: Chemistry
description: The basic understanding of chemical principles and formulae.

narrativeAdvantages.summary: Knows enough chemical laboratory procedures for analyzing and synthesizing chemical compounds to be a competent assistant.

interdependentKnowledge.summary: Foundation training required for Chemistry - Analytical and other advanced chemical work.

name: Chemistry - Analytical

synergies.description: Computer operation is highly suggested but not required.

description: Training in chemical engineering theories useful in the analysis of compounds and their practical applications.

narrativeAdvantages.summary: Highly skilled in the use of laboratory equipment and can analyze and synthesize chemicals.

name: Math: Advanced
description: Knowledge of all basic and advanced mathematics.

narrativeAdvantages.summary: Includes algebra, geometry, trigonometry, calculus, and techniques for advanced mathematical formulae.

interdependentKnowledge.summary: Encompasses all basic mathematics in addition to advanced topics.

narrativeAdvantages.summary: Includes the ability to count, addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and fractions.

conditionalRelatedSkills.summary: +2% bonus to Barter when the character also has Math: Basic.
